reasoning:
The question asks if there will be more than 10x the baseline number of riots in Maldives in the 30 days before March 8, 2026. Because the Maldives has a very low historical baseline for ACLED-coded 'Riots', the baseline effectively zeroes in on a tiny denominator. The prompt notes that the reference value on Feb 19, 2026, was 1.167. A reference value of ~1.167 mathematically translates to 1 + (x/12) = 1.167 => x/12 = 0.167 => x ≈ 2. This implies there were roughly 2 'Riots' over the prior 360 days.

To fulfill the resolution conditions, there must be at least 10 * 1.167 ≈ 11.67 => 12 'Riots' in the 30-day window from Feb 6, 2026, to Mar 8, 2026. 

At the time of this forecast (March 1, 2026), 23 days of this 30-day window have already passed. Multiple targeted searches for February 2026 unrest, protests, riots, or any violence in the Maldives yield absolutely no current events. There was a wave of anti-government protests in late 2025, but those concluded by November 2025 without generating enough violent events to meaningfully inflate the typical riot baseline. In the absence of any ongoing crisis or even isolated violent events in the news currently, it is exceptionally unlikely that ACLED will code 12 or more riots during this 30-day window. The probability is set at 2% (the hard minimum threshold) due to the complete lack of requisite events.
